source_title: AlphaVent to Omega Rotator Cuff Repair surgical technique guide
text: AlphaVent to Omega rotator cuff repair Surgical technique guide AlphaVent to Omega rotator cuff repair surgical procedure Through the lateral portal, align the AlphaVent awl perpendicular to the bone at the articular margin. Mallet the awl down to the laser line and remove. Repeat with a second AlphaVent anchor. Bring the AlphaVent anchor in through the lateral portal and place it perpendicular over the pilot hole. Rotate clockwise until the anchor’s proximal surface is flush with the bone. The dual thread technology offers tactile feedback during cortical thread engagement. Release the sutures from the cleat. Pull straight back on the inserter handle to remove. Note: AlphaVent PN 3910947021 is utilized for this specific technique. Additional offerings are listed at the end of this guide. To pass the AlphaVent sutures through the rotator cuff tissue, bring two tails of the 1.4mm XBraid TT from the anterior anchor through the lateral portal. Load the end of the passing loop into the Champion suture passer. Through the loop, load the two suture tails. Step one Step three Step two Step four AlphaVent to Omega rotator cuff repair surgical procedure Advance the passing loop through the cuff and pull the two suture tails through with the loop. Bring two tails of the 1.4mm XBraid TT from the anterior anchor through the lateral portal. Tie them down to achieve additional medial fixation. Repeat for the posterior anchor. Repeat this process for the remaining suture tails. Retrieve an untied suture tail from each anchor and a tied suture tail from each anchor through the lateral portal. If needed, the passing loop may be used prophylactically to complete a dog ear reduction. Step five Step seven Step six Step eight Load these sutures into the Omega eyelet using the pull tab. With the distal tip of the screwdriver, follow the guiding suture path down to the aperture of the hole. Place the tip of the screw over the pilot hole. Position the tip of the eyelet on the greater tuberosity. Mallet the inserter until the distal edge of the laser line is flush with the bone. Pull straight back to remove the eyelet inserter, releasing the eyelet in the bone. Note: The eyelet inserter is self-punching. However, if the surgeon determines a pilot hole is needed, instrumentation, including awls, drills, and taps, is available. See IFU P44444 for pilot hole creation instructions. While maintaining slight downward pressure on the screwdriver, pull tension on the suture limbs one at a time. Depending on the quality of bone, when pulling tension, you may feel the eyelet reposition under the cortex. The eyelet’s proprietary design leverages bone density to guide its repositioning so that the eyelet may fixate in bone.1 Step nine Step eleven Step ten Step twelve AlphaVent to Omega rotator cuff repair surgical procedure Once the suture is tensioned, hold the black handle of the screwdriver, and simultaneously turn the translucent knob in the clockwise direction. Advance the screw until its proximal surface is flush with the bone. Pull straight back to remove the screwdriver. Complete the repair with an additional Omega anchor. Step thirteen Step fourteen AlphaVent to Omega rotator cuff repair surgical procedure 6 Omega self-punching knotless anchor system Part number Description 3910500391 Omega 3.9mm PEEK knotless anchor system, single 3910500392 Omega 3.9mm PEEK knotless anchor system, double-double 3910500471 Omega 4.75mm PEEK knotless anchor system, single 3910500472 Omega 4.75mm PEEK knotless anchor system, double-double 3910500652 Omega 6.5mm PEEK knotless anchor and eyelet Omega instrumentation 3910947204 3.9mm Omega drill 3910947205 3.9mm Omega tap (with handle) 3910947206 4.75mm Omega tap (with handle) 3910947212 Drill guide 3910500394 3.9mm Omega tap 3910500395 3.9mm Omega awl 3910500474 4.75mm Omega tap 0234020158 Multi purpose T-handle 0234020117 Ratcheting driver handle AlphaVent and Omega shared instrumentation 3910947200 3.9mm Omega and 4.75mm AlphaVent awl 3910947201 4.75mm Omega and AlphaVent awl 3910947202 4.75mm Omega and AlphaVent drill 3910947203 5.5mm AlphaVent and 4.75mm hard bone Omega drill XBraid Passing Loop Part number Description 3910900043 1.4mm XBraid TT, white/blue, passing loop suture tape, non-needled, 24" suture with 5" loop 3910900044 1.0mm XBraid TT, blue/black, passing loop suture tape, non-needled, 24" suture with 7" loop 3910900040 #2 XBraid S, white/blue, passing loop suture, non-needled, 24" suture with 1.5" loop AlphaVent hard-body anchor system Part number Description 4.75mm suture anchors 3910947022 4.75mm suture anchor, PEEK, 2 strands of #2 XBraid S 3910947032 4.75mm suture anchor, PEEK, 3 strands of #2 XBraid S 3910947132 4.75mm suture anchor, PEEK, 3 strands of #2 XBraid S with needles 3910947021 4.75mm suture anchor, PEEK, 2 strands of 1.4mm XBraid TT 3910947031 4.75mm suture anchor, PEEK, 3 strands of 1.4mm XBraid TT 3910947131 4.75mm suture anchor, PEEK, 3 strands of 1.4mm XBraid TT with needles 3910947020 4.75mm suture anchor, PEEK, 2 strands of 1.8mm XBraid TT 3910947120 4.75mm suture anchor, PEEK, 2 strands of 1.8mm
